pc.name: Mary Blackwood
pc.special: Once per voyage, pull a shipmate back from the brink of death.

Surgeon.special: Once per voyage, pull a shipmate back from the brink of death.

Bosun.special: You can drive the crew to a feat of seamanship no one thought possible.

Naturalist.special: Ashore, you notice the one thing that changes everything.

Powder_Monkey.special: Small and quick, you go where no grown sailor can.

Chaplain.special: Your words can pull a man back from mutiny or despair.

Master.special: You always know where the ship truly is.

days.narration: Since the last glass off the Bay of Biscay, the people in good heart. HMS Endeavour departs Plymouth with 94 souls to observe the Transit of Venus. First part fresh Breezes and Cloudy, remainder little wind and Clear. At 2 p.m. got under Sail and put to Sea, having on board 94 Persons, including Officers, Seamen, Gentlemen, and their Servants; near 18 Months' Provisions, 10 Carriage Guns, 12 Swivels, with good Store of Ammunition and Stores of all kinds. … Tupaia won at dice off Joseph Banks. Dr. Daniel Solander won at dice off Charles Green. John Ravenhill won at dice off Charles Green. Robert Molyneux shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks. John Ravenhill shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks.

Berthed the Ship's Company, Mustered the Chests and Stove all that were unnecessary. John Gore shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks. Mary Blackwood spun a yarn with John Gore on the fo'c'sle.

days.narration: This day off the Bay of Biscay, the people in good heart. Former part fresh Gales and Hazey with rain; remainder a Moderate breeze and Cloudy. Wind Easterly; course South 48 degrees West; distance 130 miles; latitude 47 degrees 16 minutes North, longitude 9 degrees 7 minutes West; at noon Lizard North 40 degrees 5 minutes East; 69 leagues. Joseph Banks won at dice off James Magra.

At 1/2 past 1 p.m. Spoke with His Majesty's Ship Guardaloupe; at 6 Close Reeft the Topsails, and got down the Top Gallant Yards. Wind Westerly; course South 27 degrees West; distance 33 miles; latitude 46 degrees 9 minutes North, longitude 9 degrees 52 minutes West; … Lt. James Cook won at dice off John Ravenhill. Zachary Hicks won at dice off Mary Blackwood.

days.narration: With the ship becalmed off the Bay of Biscay, the people in good heart. First and Middle parts, Moderate breezes and Clear; Latter, fresh Gales and Cloudy. At 6 p.m. loosed the 2nd Reef out of the Topsails, and at 8 a.m. took them in again; at Noon Tacked and stood to the North-West, having stood before to the Southward. Wind West to South-West; course South 36 degrees East; … John Gore won at dice off Joseph Banks. Charles Green won at dice off Nicholas Young. Joseph Banks spun a yarn with Mary Blackwood on the fo'c'sle. Mary Blackwood spun a yarn with Nicholas Young on the fo'c'sle.

days.narration: This day off the Bay of Biscay, the people in good heart. Very hard gales, with some heavy showers of Rain, the most part of these 24 Hours, which brought us under our two Courses, Broke one of our Main Topmast phuttock Plates, washed overboard a small Boat belonging to the Boatswain, and drowned between 3 and 4 Dozen of our Poultry, which was worst of all. … Lt. James Cook won at dice off Jonathan Monkhouse. Zachary Hicks won at dice off Stephen Forwood. Jonathan Monkhouse shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks. Mary Blackwood spun a yarn with Charles Green on the fo'c'sle.

days.narration: This day off Cape Finister and Cape Ortugal, the people in good heart. Fresh Gales and Cloudy the most part of these 24 hours. P.M. got up the spare Mainsail to dry, it being Wet by the Water getting into the Sail room, occasioned by the Ship being very Leakey in her upper works. At 5 a.m. … Sam Evans won at dice off Tom Rossiter. James Magra spun a yarn with Mary Blackwood on the fo'c'sle. Mary Blackwood spun a yarn with Dr. Daniel Solander on the fo'c'sle.

days.narration: These four-and-twenty hours off the Bay of Biscay, the people in good heart. First part little wind and Hazey, with rain; remainder strong Gales with hard squalls, which brought us under our close Reeft Topsails, and obliged us to strike Topgallant Yards. At 8 a.m. wore ship and stood to the Southward. Wind South-West and West; course South 68 degrees 45 minutes West; distance 44 miles; … Charles Green won at dice off Sam Evans. Jonathan Monkhouse won at dice off Tupaia. Zachary Hicks won at dice off Sam Evans. Tupaia shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks.

days.place: Island of Porto Santo bore North
days.narration: These four-and-twenty hours off Island of Porto Santo bore North, the people in good heart. Moderate breezes and fine Clear weather. At 6 a.m. the Island of Porto Santo bore North-West by West, distance 9 or 10 leagues. Hauld the Wind to the westward at noon, the Deserters extending from West-South-West to South-West by South, the Body of Madeira West 1/2 South, and Porto Santo North-North-West 1/2 West. … Dr. Daniel Solander won at dice off Lt. James Cook. Zachary Hicks won at dice off James Magra. Tom Rossiter spun a yarn with Mary Blackwood on the fo'c'sle.

days.place: Island of Teneriff West by North
days.narration: Under a brassy sky off Island of Teneriff West by North, the people in good heart. A fresh Breeze and Clear weather the most part of these 24 Hours. I take this to be the North-East Trade we have now got into. At 6 p.m. the North-East end of the Island of Teneriff West by North, distance 3 or 4 Leagues. Off this North-East point lies some Rocks high above the water. … Richard Orton won at dice off Robert Molyneux.

days.narration: This day off the Atlantic crossing, the people in good heart. Light breezes of Wind, sometimes Clear and sometimes Cloudy weather. At 1/2 past 3 double reeft the Topsails, and hauld in for a Bay, wherein their appear'd to be good Anchorage, and into which I had thought of going with the Ship; … Charles Green went ashore at bay I have named Dusky Bay. days.narration: With the ship pitching hard off island or not, the people in good heart. days.region: the east coast of New Holland
days.narration: Under a clear sky off Cape Howe, the people in good heart. In the P.M. and most part of the night had a fresh Gale Westerly, with Squalls, attended with Showers of rain. In the A.M. had the Wind at South-West, with Severe weather. At 1 p.m. saw 3 Water Spouts at once; 2 were between us and the Shore, and one at some distance upon our Larboard Quarter. … Richard Orton swore he saw a beast that hopped like a hare and stood like a man. In the P.M. we steer'd along shore North-West 1/2 West, at the distance of 2 Leagues from the Main, having 9 and 10 fathoms regular soundings. … Lt. James Cook went ashore at Bay about. Dr. Daniel Solander went ashore at Bay about. Sam Evans went ashore at Bay about. John Ravenhill won at dice off Robert Molyneux. Sam Evans won at dice off Richard Orton.

days.narration: These four-and-twenty hours off Island with the Ship, the people in good heart. Winds at South-South-East and South; first part light Airs, the remainder a Gentle breeze. John Gore went ashore at Island with the Ship. John Ravenhill went ashore at Island with the Ship. Ashore at Island with the Ship, Charles Green beheld sights no shipmate would believe. Dr. Daniel Solander won at dice off James Magra.

days.region: the east coast of New Holland
days.narration: Under a brassy sky off Cape Grafton we found the land trend away North, the people in good heart. After hauling round Cape Grafton we found the land trend away North-West by West; 3 Miles to the Westward of the Cape is a Bay, wherein we Anchor'd, about 2 Miles from the Shore, in 4 fathoms, owsey bottom. … Mary Blackwood went ashore at Cape Grafton we found the land trend away North. Something went missing at Cape Grafton we found the land trend away North; suspicion fell on Jonathan Monkhouse. John Gore won at dice off Lt. James Cook. Sam Evans won at dice off Robert Molyneux. Jonathan Monkhouse won at dice off Nicholas Young.

days.place: cape from total shipwreck is very graphic
days.narration: This day off cape from total shipwreck is very graphic, the people in good heart. While Mr. Hicks was Examining the opening we struggled hard with the flood, sometime gaining a little and at other times loosing. Mary Blackwood won at dice off Sam Evans. Mary Blackwood shared grog with Dr. Daniel Solander below decks. Tupaia spun a yarn with Mary Blackwood on the fo'c'sle.

days.narration: Since the last glass off Island of Timor, the people in good heart. Variable light Airs and Clear weather. Steer'd North-West, in order to discover the Land plainer until 4 in the morning, at which time the wind came to North-West and West, with which we stood to the Southward until 9 o'Clock, when we Tack'd and stood North-West, having the wind at West-South-West. … John Ravenhill won at dice off Dr. Daniel Solander. John Gore won at dice off Joseph Banks. Richard Orton won at dice off James Magra. Mary Blackwood shared grog with Jonathan Monkhouse below decks.

days.narration: Since the last glass off Torres Strait, the company steady enough. Stood in shore, with a light breeze at South by West until 1/2 past 5 o'Clock in the P.M., when, being a Mile and a 1/2 from the Shore, and in 16 fathoms, we tack'd and stood off. At this time the Extreams of the Land extended from North-East by East to West by South 1/2 South; … Charles Green fell from the rigging and lies stove-in below. Joseph Banks won at dice off John Gore. Robert Molyneux won at dice off Charles Green. Robert Molyneux won at dice off Lt. James Cook. James Magra won at dice off John Ravenhill.

days.place: Island of Rotte extending as far to the Southward as South
days.narration: With the ship close-hauled off Island of Rotte extending as far to the Southward as South, the company steady enough. Winds Easterly, with which we steer'd West-North-West until 2 o'Clock, when being pretty near the North end of Rotte, we hauled up North-North-West, in order to go between it and Anaboa. After steering 3 Leagues upon this Course we edged away North-West by West, and by 6 we were clear of all the Islands; … Lt. James Cook shows the scurvy — loose teeth and dark blotches — despite Cook's sauerkraut. Zachary Hicks won at dice off Stephen Forwood. James Magra won at dice off Jonathan Monkhouse. Mary Blackwood shared grog with John Gore below decks.

days.place: Island hath no place in any of our Charts
days.narration: This day off Island hath no place in any of our Charts, the company steady enough. Winds at South-South-East, South-East, and East; a gentle breeze, which we steer'd West-South-West by Compass. At 4 o'Clock we discover'd a small low Island* (* Dama Island.) bearing South-South-West, distant 3 Leagues. … Charles Green won at dice off Lt. James Cook. Lt. James Cook won at dice off Joseph Banks. Dr. Daniel Solander won at dice off Sam Evans. Zachary Hicks won at dice off Stephen Forwood. Jonathan Monkhouse won at dice off James Magra.

days.narration: Under a leaden sky off Torres Strait, the company steady enough. Winds Easterly; a moderate breeze, which by noon brought us into the Latitude of 11 degrees 10 minutes South, Longitude 240 degrees 48 minutes West. Course and distance saild since yesterday at noon is West, 8 miles. Tupaia won at dice off Charles Green. Mary Blackwood won at dice off Jonathan Monkhouse. John Ravenhill shared grog with Mary Blackwood below decks.

days.narration: Since the last glass off the homeward Atlantic, the people in good heart. Little wind and hazey weather. Swell from the Northward. Wind North-North-West to South-West; course North 46 degrees 45 minutes East; distance 43 miles; latitude 46 degrees 45 minutes North, longitude 8 degrees 54 minutes West. John Gore won at dice off Stephen Forwood. Jonathan Monkhouse won at dice off Stephen Forwood. Joseph Banks won at dice off Jonathan Monkhouse.

A swell from the North-North-West all day. Wind South Westerly; course North 21 degrees East; distance 100 miles; …

days.narration: These four-and-twenty hours off the homeward Atlantic, the people in good heart. The Endeavour nears home after nearly three years, a charted Pacific, and a third of the company dead. Pleasant breezes and Clear weather. At 6 o'Clock in the Morning sounded, and Struck ground in 60 fathoms Shells and Stones, by which I judged we were the length of Scilly Isles. At Noon we saw land from the Mast Head, bearing North, which we judged to be about the Land's End. Soundings 54 fathoms, Coarse, Grey Sand. … Robert Molyneux won at dice off Charles Green. Stephen Forwood won at dice off Jonathan Monkhouse.
